title: Billy Butler (guitarist)

infobox.value: (December 15, 1924 – March 20, 1991) was an American [soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz") guitarist.[[1]](#cite_note-bare-1)
* *Music for Dancing The Twist!* ([RCA Victor](/wiki/RCA_Victor_Records "RCA Victor Records"), 1961)

* *[Blue Pyramid](/wiki/Blue_Pyramid_(Johnny_Hodges_and_Wild_Bill_Davis_album) "Blue Pyramid (Johnny Hodges and Wild Bill Davis album)")* ([Verve](/wiki/Verve_Records "Verve Records"), 1966) - with [Wild Bill Davis](/wiki/Wild_Bill_Davis "Wild Bill Davis")
* *Freddie King is a Blues Master* ([Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Records "Atlantic Records"), 1969)
* *[Legend of the Blues Vol. 1](/wiki/Legend_of_the_Blues_Vol._1 "Legend of the Blues Vol. 1")* ([Jubilee](/wiki/Jubilee_Records "Jubilee Records"), 1967)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American soul jazz guitarist (1924–1991)

| Billy Butler | |
| --- | --- |
| [Billy Butler in 1977](/wiki/File:Billy_Butler.jpg "Billy Butler in 1977") Billy Butler in 1977 | |
| Background information | |
| Born | (1924-12-15)December 15, 1924  [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia"), Pennsylvania, U.S. |
| Died | March 20, 1991(1991-03-20) (aged 66)  [Teaneck](/wiki/Teaneck,_New_Jersey "Teaneck, New Jersey"), New Jersey |
| Genres | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| Occupation | Musician |
| Instrument | Guitar |
| Formerly of | [Bill Doggett](/wiki/Bill_Doggett "Bill Doggett") |

Musical artist

**William Butler Jr.** (December 15, 1924 – March 20, 1991) was an American [soul jazz](/wiki/Soul_jazz "Soul jazz") guitarist.[[1]](#cite_note-bare-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Billy_Butler_(guitarist)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

A native of Philadelphia,[[1]](#cite_note-bare-1) Butler began his career in the 1940s behind the Harlemaires. In the 1950s he was a member of a trio led by Doc Bagby and accompanied keyboardist [Bill Doggett](/wiki/Bill_Doggett "Bill Doggett").[[2]](#cite_note-Great-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Wynn-3) He co-wrote "[Honky Tonk](/wiki/Honky_Tonk_(song) "Honky Tonk (song)")", an R&B hit for Doggett.[[2]](#cite_note-Great-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Wynn-3)

Butler also worked with [Al Casey](/wiki/Al_Casey_(jazz_guitarist) "Al Casey (jazz guitarist)"), [King Curtis](/wiki/King_Curtis "King Curtis"), [Eddie "Lockjaw" Davis](/wiki/Eddie_%22Lockjaw%22_Davis "Eddie \"Lockjaw\" Davis"), [Bill Davison](/wiki/Bill_Davison "Bill Davison"), [Tommy Flanagan](/wiki/Tommy_Flanagan_(musician) "Tommy Flanagan (musician)"), [Panama Francis](/wiki/Panama_Francis "Panama Francis"), [Dizzy Gillespie](/wiki/Dizzy_Gillespie "Dizzy Gillespie"), [Benny Goodman](/wiki/Benny_Goodman "Benny Goodman"), [Johnny Hodges](/wiki/Johnny_Hodges "Johnny Hodges"), [Floyd "Candy" Johnson](/wiki/Floyd_%22Candy%22_Johnson "Floyd \"Candy\" Johnson"), [David "Fathead" Newman](/wiki/David_%22Fathead%22_Newman "David \"Fathead\" Newman"), [Houston Person](/wiki/Houston_Person "Houston Person"), [Sammy Price](/wiki/Sammy_Price "Sammy Price"), [Jimmy Smith](/wiki/Jimmy_Smith_(musician) "Jimmy Smith (musician)"), [Norris Turney](/wiki/Norris_Turney "Norris Turney"), and [Dinah Washington](/wiki/Dinah_Washington "Dinah Washington").[[3]](#cite_note-Wynn-3)[[4]](#cite_note-nyo-4)

He is credited as the guitarist on [Joey Dee and the Starliters](/wiki/Joey_Dee_and_the_Starliters "Joey Dee and the Starliters")' "[Peppermint Twist, Parts 1 & 2](/wiki/Peppermint_Twist "Peppermint Twist")" recorded in September 1961 at the Peppermint Lounge in New York City. Part 1 of the song went to the top of the Billboard pop charts in January 1962.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Butler died of a heart attack at home in [Teaneck, New Jersey](/wiki/Teaneck,_New_Jersey "Teaneck, New Jersey"), in 1991.[[1]](#cite_note-bare-1)

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Billy_Butler_(guitarist)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Discography")]

### As leader

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Billy_Butler_(guitarist)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: As leader")]

* *[This Is Billy Butler!](/wiki/This_Is_Billy_Butler! "This Is Billy Butler!")* ([Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), 1969)
* *[Guitar Soul!](/wiki/Guitar_Soul! "Guitar Soul!")* (Prestige, 1969)
* *[Yesterday, Today & Tomorrow](/wiki/Yesterday,_Today_%26_Tomorrow_(Billy_Butler_album) "Yesterday, Today & Tomorrow (Billy Butler album)")* (Prestige, 1970)
* *[Night Life](/wiki/Night_Life_(Billy_Butler_album) "Night Life (Billy Butler album)")* (Prestige, 1971)
* *Billy Butler Plays Via Galactica* (Kilmarnock, 1973)
* *Guitar Odyssey* with [Al Casey](/wiki/Al_Casey_(jazz_guitarist) "Al Casey (jazz guitarist)"), Jackie Williams (Jazz Odyssey, 1974)
* *Don't Be That Way* ([Black & Blue](/wiki/Black_%26_Blue_Records "Black & Blue Records"), 1976)

### As sideman

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Billy_Butler_(guitarist)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: As sideman")]

**With [Gene Ammons](/wiki/Gene_Ammons "Gene Ammons")**

* *[Brother Jug!](/wiki/Brother_Jug! "Brother Jug!")* ([Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records"), 1969)
* *[My Way](/wiki/My_Way_(Gene_Ammons_album) "My Way (Gene Ammons album)")* (Prestige, 1971)

**With [King Curtis](/wiki/King_Curtis "King Curtis")**

* *It's Party Time with King Curtis* (Tru-Sound, 1962)
* *Doing the Dixie Twist* (Tru-Sound, 1962)

**With [Bill Doggett](/wiki/Bill_Doggett "Bill Doggett")**

* *Hot Doggett* (King 395–514, 1956)

* *[Everybody Dance the Honky Tonk](/wiki/Everybody_Dance_the_Honky_Tonk "Everybody Dance the Honky Tonk")* (King 395–531, 1956)
* *[Dame Dreaming](/wiki/Dame_Dreaming "Dame Dreaming")* (King, 1957)
* *[A Salute to Ellington](/wiki/A_Salute_to_Ellington "A Salute to Ellington")* (King, 1957)
* *[Doggett Beat for Dancing Feet](/wiki/Doggett_Beat_for_Dancing_Feet "Doggett Beat for Dancing Feet")* (King, 1957)
* *Candle Glow* (King 563, 1958)
* *Swingin' Easy* (King 582, 1958)
* *[Dance Awhile with Doggett](/wiki/Dance_Awhile_with_Doggett "Dance Awhile with Doggett")* (King 585, 1958)
* *12 Songs of Christmas* (King 600, 1958)
* *Hold It!* (King 609, 1959)
* *High and Wide* (King 633, 1959)
* *Big City Dance Party* (King 641, 1959)
* *Bill Doggett on Tour* [not a live album] (King 667, 1959)
* *For Reminiscent Lovers, Romantic Songs By Bill Doggett* (King 706, 1960)
* *Back With More Bill Doggett* (King 723, 1960)
* *The Many Moods of Bill Doggett* (King 778, 1962)

* *Impressions* (King 868, 1963)
* *Wow!* ([ABC-Paramount](/wiki/ABC_Records "ABC Records"), 1964)

**With [Dizzy Gillespie](/wiki/Dizzy_Gillespie "Dizzy Gillespie")**

* *[The Melody Lingers On](/wiki/The_Melody_Lingers_On_(Dizzy_Gillespie_album) "The Melody Lingers On (Dizzy Gillespie album)")* ([Limelight](/wiki/Limelight_Records "Limelight Records"), 1966)
* *[It's My Way](/wiki/It%27s_My_Way_(Dizzy_Gillespie_album) "It's My Way (Dizzy Gillespie album)")* ([Solid State](/wiki/Solid_State_Records_(jazz_label) "Solid State Records (jazz label)"), 1969)
* *[Cornucopia](/wiki/Cornucopia_(album) "Cornucopia (album)")* (Solid State, 1969)

**With [John P. Hammond](/wiki/John_P._Hammond "John P. Hammond")**

* *Big City Blues* ([Vanguard](/wiki/Vanguard_Records "Vanguard Records"), 1964)

**With [Johnny Hodges](/wiki/Johnny_Hodges "Johnny Hodges")**

* *[Blue Pyramid](/wiki/Blue_Pyramid_(Johnny_Hodges_and_Wild_Bill_Davis_album) "Blue Pyramid (Johnny Hodges and Wild Bill Davis album)")* ([Verve](/wiki/Verve_Records "Verve Records"), 1966) - with [Wild Bill Davis](/wiki/Wild_Bill_Davis "Wild Bill Davis")
* *[Triple Play](/wiki/Triple_Play_(Johnny_Hodges_album) "Triple Play (Johnny Hodges album)")* (RCA Victor, 1967)

**With [Illinois Jacquet](/wiki/Illinois_Jacquet "Illinois Jacquet")**

* *[Spectrum](/wiki/Spectrum_(Illinois_Jacquet_album) "Spectrum (Illinois Jacquet album)")* ([Argo](/wiki/Argo_Records "Argo Records"), 1965)

**With [Ben E. King](/wiki/Ben_E._King "Ben E. King")**

**With [Freddie King](/wiki/Freddie_King "Freddie King")**

* *Freddie King is a Blues Master* ([Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Records "Atlantic Records"), 1969)

**With [Memphis Slim](/wiki/Memphis_Slim "Memphis Slim")**

* *[Legend of the Blues Vol. 1](/wiki/Legend_of_the_Blues_Vol._1 "Legend of the Blues Vol. 1")* ([Jubilee](/wiki/Jubilee_Records "Jubilee Records"), 1967)
* *[The Legacy of the Blues Vol. 7](/wiki/The_Legacy_of_the_Blues_Vol._7 "The Legacy of the Blues Vol. 7")* ([Sonet](/wiki/Sonet_Records "Sonet Records"), 1973)

**With [Houston Person](/wiki/Houston_Person "Houston Person")**

**With [Jimmy Smith](/wiki/Jimmy_Smith_(musician) "Jimmy Smith (musician)")**

**With [Sonny Stitt](/wiki/Sonny_Stitt "Sonny Stitt")**

* *[Goin' Down Slow](/wiki/Goin%27_Down_Slow_(album) "Goin' Down Slow (album)")* (Prestige, 1972)

**With [Grady Tate](/wiki/Grady_Tate "Grady Tate")**

**With others**

* *[Oliver Nelson Plays Michelle](/wiki/Oliver_Nelson_Plays_Michelle "Oliver Nelson Plays Michelle")*, [Oliver Nelson](/wiki/Oliver_Nelson "Oliver Nelson") ([Impulse!](/wiki/Impulse!_Records "Impulse! Records"), 1966)

* *[That Healin' Feelin'](/wiki/That_Healin%27_Feelin%27_(Richard_%22Groove%22_Holmes_album) "That Healin' Feelin' (Richard \"Groove\" Holmes album)")*, [Richard "Groove" Holmes](/wiki/Richard_Holmes_(organist) "Richard Holmes (organist)") (Prestige, 1968)

* *What's the Secret of Your Success*, [The Coasters](/wiki/The_Coasters "The Coasters") (Mr. R&B, 1980) - compilation

* *The Glory of Alberta Hunter*, [Alberta Hunter](/wiki/Alberta_Hunter "Alberta Hunter") (Columbia, 1982)
* *[Blues 'N' Jazz](/wiki/Blues_%27N%27_Jazz "Blues 'N' Jazz")*, [B.B. King](/wiki/B.B._King "B.B. King") ([MCA](/wiki/MCA_Records "MCA Records"), 1983)
* *Look for the Silver Lining*, Alberta Hunter (Columbia, 1983)
* *[One More for the Road](/wiki/One_More_for_the_Road_(Charles_Brown_album) "One More for the Road (Charles Brown album)")*, [Charles Brown](/wiki/Charles_Brown_(musician) "Charles Brown (musician)") ([Alligator](/wiki/Alligator_Records "Alligator Records"), 1986)[[6]](#cite_note-AM_credits-6)
